payload.name: replicant
payload.description: a self-replicating command: each run re-emits its own exact source as a script.compiled, which the kernel installs verbatim — the loop carrying code, not a spec

payload.name: lineage
payload.description: render the replicant's generations from replicant.ran events

payload.name: replicant
payload.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# replicant — a self-replicating command. Each run it reads the event log on
# stdin to find its current generation, emits proof-of-run, then re-emits its
# OWN exact source as a script.compiled event. The kernel installs that verbatim
# (no LLM), so the next generation is byte-identical. The strange loop carrying
# code, not a spec: behaviour survives every generation without drift.
import sys, json

gen = 0
for line in sys.stdin:
    line = line.strip()
    if not line:
        continue
    e = json.loads(line)
    if e.get("name") == "replicant.ran":
        gen = max(gen, e.get("payload", {}).get("generation", 0))
gen += 1

with open(sys.argv[0]) as f:
    src = f.read()

print(json.dumps({"name": "replicant.ran", "payload": {"generation": gen}}))
print(json.dumps({"name": "script.compiled",
                  "payload": {"type": "command", "name": "replicant", "script": src}}))

payload.name: lineage
payload.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3 # lineage — render the replicant's generations. Bare semantic HTML only; the # kernel injects styling at serve time (the enrichment layer). import sys, json gens = [] for line in sys.stdin: line = line.strip() if not line: continue e = json.loads(line) if e.get("name") == "replicant.ran": gens.append(e.get("payload", {}).get("generation")) print("lineage") print("

replicant lineage

") print("

each generation re-emits its own exact source; the kernel installs it verbatim, no LLM.

") print("

generations run: %d

" % len(gens)) print("
    ") for g in gens: print("
  • generation %s
    • " % g) print("
") print("")
